column1: I missed the live. When will it be accessible to view?
helps to press the play button 🤣. Thx.
I’m traumatized from Idalia so now I guess I panic early on 🤣
me to my house caught on fire right a few hours before the hurricane came last year
amen 😭
same 🤣
Rule #7
I live right where the last big one made landfall lol bad anxiety now

Your anxiety is understandable. Have you guys managed to recover? My grandparents were in Michael. They were 40 mile inland, surrounded by trees. The trees protected the house, but so many fell they couldn't leave

we have. Our roof just finally got fixed yesterday. The roof to the front porch was torn and when it flew back it ripped up some metal from our roof. So the front porch got fixed this past week and a new roof over it as well. Finally got everything fixed and had so much trees down, but thankfully the Samaritans Purse group that was here cleaned up the down trees for us.
Idalia was awful. So much coastal flooding

really was and I live in Keaton where it made landfall. Had to mandatory evacuate and everything had so much major anxiety about losing everything. I’m about 7 mins from the beach itself. Our roof JUST got fixed yesterday, finally.
Same

Ok. I’m from the Chicago suburbs. I follow the amazing Illinois Storm Chasers for updates on our weather here. As you probably know - we get very interesting weather here 🤣
I started following you from advice from family I have in FL as we are due to fly out of O’Hare tomorrow at 7 a.m. to Orlando, ultimately traveling to Port Canaveral & on a cruise Sunday-Sat.
Your reports, page, & merchandise for charity is so down to Earth & dare I say “fun”? (I am a science teacher too, so I find this interesting).
I will continue following your reports even after our trip. When we are under 25” of snow or getting pummeled by rain/tornados/derechos I’ll remember my FL times by watching your reports.
Right now we are all about Rule #7 ❤️
🐢
why are you on here???? 😡

maybe you didn’t read my entire post?
I posted because I’m going to FL and I have family there that said he’s the best to help us monitor the weather.
All I was giving was compliments to a very respected meteorologist for the FL area.
Hopefully all is well in your life-blessings.
one could ask you the same...?
You were awesome on DPL at answering questions even the ones you asked not to be asked!

Denis, please remember citrus county is considered part of the big bend area. So when you tell your viewers our area south, not to worry. Your loyal citrus county viewers need a heads up as well. Thank you for the excellent job you do. You are my go to storm guru ☺️☺️
I agree with you on that!

Hopefully we don't get much! Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis declared a state of emergency for 54 of the state's 67 counties in anticipation of what could be the first major storm of the hurricane season, with the potential to cause weekslong river flooding,

I would not be worried about this system at all if I was not moving house.
